verdicts.note: The sole evidence is one clause — 'limits on surveillance technology and data sharing with federal agencies' — which the dossier files under public safety and which never mentions immigration, ICE, or sanctuary policy. Reading it as an immigration-enforcement position is indirect inference (low). Same clause is also double-counted for the tech axis.

Same single surveillance clause recycled from the safety platform; the dossier documents no position on AI, data centers, or tech-platform regulation. Extrapolating a general AI/big-tech posture from one police-surveillance item is indirect inference — mirrors the tech adjustment for Street in the LD37 Senate race.
